result:
Good, that's all standard Next.js scaffolding. Everything's in place and verified working. Here's a summary of what I found and fixed:

## What was broken

This site is the aftermath of a rebrand — **InboxZap** (an email-cleanup tool) pivoted to **Lumina** (an AI meeting assistant) — and the SEO surface never caught up:

1. **Title/meta tags still said InboxZap** while the homepage content was 100% Lumina. Anyone finding the site via a branded "inboxzap" search (which was actually converting at 4.38% CTR, the best-performing query) landed on a page about meeting notes with no explanation — a classic post-click bounce driver.
2. **`/pricing` 404'd** — despite being the homepage's primary "Start free" CTA target and a link in the sitemap.
3. **`/features` 404'd** — also linked from nav and the sitemap.
4. **The blog was fully broken**: it fetched from `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist anywhere in the repo — every crawler and user just saw "Could not load posts." Meanwhile GSC showed the old `/blog/post-1..12` URLs (now gone) had pulled in thousands of impressions.
5. **`/faq` 301-redirected to `/#faq`**, but no `#faq` section existed on the homepage — a redirect to nothing.
6. Sitemap referenced two 404ing URLs and explicitly excluded blog content via a TODO.
7. OG image pointed to `/img/og-inboxzap.png`, a file that doesn't exist in `public/`.

## What I changed
- Fixed all metadata (title template, description, OG/Twitter) to match Lumina, added `metadataBase` + canonicals per page.
- Built real `/features` and `/pricing` pages.
- Rebuilt the blog as server-rendered/statically-generated (`generateStaticParams`), added 4 substantive on-brand posts targeting meeting-notes/async-work keywords, with Article structured data.
- Added a real FAQ section (`id="faq"`, FAQPage schema) plus a brief "InboxZap is now Lumina" note so branded searchers aren't confused.
- 301-redirected legacy `/blog/post-N` and `/app` URLs instead of leaving them 404 (preserves some crawl equity from historically-linked pages).
- Fixed the sitemap to include real, live URLs and every blog post with `lastModified`.
- Replaced the broken OG image with a dynamically generated one (`next/og`).

Verified with a full `next build` (all 13 routes compile/prerender cleanly) and a local `next start` smoke test confirming redirects, blog pages, and sitemap all work as expected.

One unrelated item worth flagging: `npm install` reported a critical CVE in `next@14.2.5`. I didn't touch it since it's outside this task's scope, but it's worth a deliberate upgrade separately.
